Summary: Social Media - LinkedIn - Insights Update Launch (Theme)
Description:
Topic: Insights Update launch — theme post on what the new Insights capability means for IC teams (read-attribution / measurement story).
Post type: static-theme
Wraps: cerkl-insights-update
Copy:
Someone asks how the all-staff update performed. How long does it take you to answer?

For a lot of internal comms teams, the honest answer is "longer than it should." You send the message, then dig through a reporting tool that was never built for a quick answer, clicking layers deep for the one number leadership wanted.

Insights changes that. It is a ground-up rebuild of the analytics inside Cerkl Broadcast, live for every Broadcast customer as of June 20, 2026.

The part that matters most for measurement: context. A 22% open rate on its own tells you nothing. Insights compares every rate against your own organization's average from the last 18 months, so you can see whether you landed above or below where your audience normally does. That is the difference between a metric and an insight.

You also get clearer visuals, faster load times, drill-down from a high-level summary to a single subscriber's click history, channel-level and News Digest reporting, and dashboards you can hand to a stakeholder without exporting a thing.

Communicators are measured on outcomes but rarely given time to be analysts. Insights closes that gap.

What's the one question about your comms you've been meaning to answer?

Link in comments ↓

[ASSET — Stat card] Stat: 18 months | Supporting: "Every rate, compared to your own running average." | Takeaway: "That's the difference between a metric and an insight." | Source: Cerkl Insights | Cerkl brand colors, one large numeral, logo bottom corner, mobile-legible.

#InternalCommunications #EmployeeEngagement #ICMeasurement

Summary: Social Media - LinkedIn - Insights Update Launch (Carousel)
Description:
Topic: Insights Update launch — carousel walking IC teams through the redesigned Broadcast analytics (what changed, why it matters for measurement). Wraps the launch announcement blog.
Post type: carousel
Wraps: cerkl-insights-update
Copy:
Measuring internal comms used to mean clicking three layers deep and losing your place before you found the number leadership asked for.

Insights is now live for every Broadcast customer. Five changes that make measurement faster. ↓

[Slide 1 — Cover] Insights is here: 5 changes to Broadcast analytics / A ground-up rebuild, live for every Broadcast customer June 20.
[Slide 2 — See it, don't dig for it] Performance now reads as a picture before you read it as a number. A wider range of visuals surfaces trends, comparisons, and outliers at a glance. Want raw detail? Customizable tables let you add, remove, and rearrange columns, then export. Why it matters: a quick visual read and a full data export on the same screen.
[Slide 3 — Navigation that gets out of your way] Insights reworked navigation from the ground up: breadcrumbs show where you are, pathways stay consistent. Load times are faster too. Why it matters: you retrace your steps instead of starting over.
[Slide 4 — From the big picture to a single click] Start at a summary, then drill into email and Blast engagement, content by channel, and audience detail down to one person and one message. Why it matters: you are never stuck at the altitude the tool decided for you.
[Slide 5 — Every rate, compared to your own norm] Insights compares current performance against your org's own 18-month average. Why it matters: the difference between a metric and an insight you can act on.
[Slide 6 — Dashboards you can actually share] Build custom dashboards, then decide who sees them. Admins get full visibility, team members share selectively, permissions adjust as needs change. Why it matters: reporting moves from your screen into the leadership conversation.
[Slide 7 — CTA] Insights is live for every Broadcast customer now. Full walkthrough in our latest post → Link in comments.

#InternalCommunications #EmployeeEngagement #HRTech
